channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Phreak Steps Away as LoL Live Designer for Secret Project
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games’ David ‘Phreak’ Turley has announced he is temporarily stepping away as the live designer for League of Legends to work on a secret project.

Phreak was a LoL esports commentator for 13 years, from 2009 until 2002, before joining Riot’s balance team.

Since then, he has worked on every patch and directly helped with various major changes, including five reworks, the 2023 Marksmen update, and this year’s objective changes.

With the release of each patch, Phreak would also upload a lengthy video walking through the entire patch notes and explaining why Riot had made these changes.

And in the latest video, previewing LoL Patch 26.7, Phreak revealed that he will no longer be releasing those breakdowns:

“This will be my last patch covering as a live designer.

“Late last year, I was asked if I wanted to work on a different project within League of Legends. I said yes, and then I basically built a plan for offboarding onto the project and off of live design.”

Phreak confirmed that he is still a LoL game designer, and while he wouldn’t share any details on what he’s working on, he claims LoL fans should be excited:

“I wanted to move over, I think it’s a very cool project, I think a lot of you will really love it. I’m very excited about this overall.”

What Could Phreak Be Working On?

The one major LoL project Riot has announced is ‘League Next’, a complete overhaul for the 17-year-old MOBA.

The update, set to release next year, will give the game an all-new client, an upgraded new-player experience, and a visual refresh for Summoner’s Rift.

But this is purely speculation, and what Phreak has been asked to work on could be something else entirely.

What changes would you like to see made to LoL? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see what else is coming to the game with this year’s LoL April Fools’ skins.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Does MOUSE: PI For Hire Have A Physical Edition? MOUSE: PI Disc Physical Copy And Disc, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Understandably, digital isn’t for everyone, as you want to own what you buy. MOUSE: PI For Hire has different editions, and a physical copy might be a make-or-break for buyers.

Fumi Games has revealed the MOUSE‘s editions, giving you the fun but tricky decision of choosing how much you want to spend and what you get for your money. This sends us down another rabbit hole: MOUSE: PI For Hire being digital is a lock, but can you play it through a disc? There is a definitive answer.

Can You Buy MOUSE: PI For Hire On Disc? Physical Edition, Explained

MOUSE: PI For Hire officially has a physical copy, allowing you to purchase MOUSE: PI For Hire on disc and own a tangible game box.

Many of us grew up owning our games and knowing nothing different. The feeling of getting a new game for Christmas or your birthday, unwrapping the shrink wrap around the game box, reading the instruction manual (yes, they were a thing), and putting it in the console.

  • More Insider Gaming: MOUSE: PI For Hire Release Date & Start Times

In 2026, digital is king across the board. Not the whole board, thankfully, and it’s important that physical media is retained. This is why Fumi Games revealed on March 23 that a physical version of MOUSE is happening.

MOUSE: PI For Hire Physical Disc Release Date

July 10 is when MOUSE: PI For Hire physical edition orders and discs are being released, and it’s only a three-month turnaround between digital and physical.

  • More Insider Gaming: MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Early Access & Demo Status, Explained

The official announcement came from the developer:

“The rumors on the street are true. MOUSE: P.I. For Hire will see a physical release across a Standard Edition and Deluxe Edition, available on July 10, 2026. Pre-orders are live now!

What are you waiting for?

How Much is MOUSE: PI For Hire?

The most basic and barebones edition of MOUSE: PI For Hire costs $29.99 or £24.99, and it goes up to $49.99 or £49.99 for the Mouseburg Deluxe Edition.

Insider Gaming has already broken down every MOUSE: PI For Hire edition and price, and what you get with each. We encourage you to check it out for a full walkthrough, giving you a good idea of what to buy

  • More Insider Gaming: Is MOUSE: PI For Hire On Xbox Game Pass?

The main thing to note is that it’s reasonably priced, and it should make MOUSE a very competitive game and accessible to buy!

Will you get a digital copy of the game? Or is a physical edition, especially with fun collectibles and memorabilia, too good to turn down? Share which version you’re getting via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Bookmark our MOUSE: PI For Hire release date countdown & start times hub to keep tabs on the long-awaited launch.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1742: Hints and Answer for March 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1742 for March 27, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, March 27, 2026

The Wordle answer for game #1742 is a bit easier than the last few, albeit still challenging. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 4.5 out of 6 guesses. That makes it a full guess easier than the 5.5-guess average from March 26.

General Wordle Hints For March 27

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: This word usually refers to a material or substance, not an action.
  • Hint 2: It’s often mentioned in historical or artistic contexts.
  • Hint 3: The thing it describes is known for being smooth and pale.
  • Hint 4: You’re more likely to see this word in descriptions or discussions than in casual conversation.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1742 for March 27, 2026.

Starter Words For March 27, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • RIVAL
  • VIGOR
  • IRONY

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1742 On March 27, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: IVORY

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

IVORY is a noun that means “the hard white substance, a variety of dentin, composing the main part of the tusks of elephants, walruses, etc.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1741 on March 26, 2026

Answer: BEFIT

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1742?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on March 28 will be Wordle Game #1743.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.title: Pragmata’s New York Looks AI-Generated on Purpose, Devs Reveal
item.content:encoded:
Pragmata developers have revealed that human developers “painstakingly worked” to provide the AI-like feel to New York.

Capcom announced earlier this month that Pragmata will be released a week early, now arriving on April 27 for Xbox Series X/S, PC, PlayStation 5, Switch 2, and PC. The title also has a demo that players can try out right now. Ahead of the release, the developer has provided insights into the game’s New York City and how they made it look AI-generated.

Pragmata Devs Created Intentional ‘AI Errors’ For the New York Stage

During a new interview with 4Gamer (via Automaton), Pragmata’s director, Cho Yonghee, and producer Naoto Oyama discussed how they intentionally made New York look AI-generated. The director stated that the stage premise was “a fake New York generated by AI”. Yonghee explained that familiar locations make it easy for players to relate, but they wanted “to make it clear that this isn’t the real New York” and desired “something slightly distorted”.

Oyama elaborated that the game’s New York “mirrors reality”, but players will see that the unique appeal arrives through “setting errors” and how things might feel out of place, such as taxis sinking into floors, or buses sprouting from walls. However, even though the premise was generative AI, developers “painstakingly worked to incorporate mechanisms that express this AI-like uncanny feel.”

The director mentioned that Pragamta’s New York includes carefully crafted, balanced “AI errors” to avoid distracting players. He discussed the fine line between shapes that appear unique and those that become too unusual to be perceived as puzzles. Achieving the right balance between distinctiveness and mere background elements was a challenge for the team.

There are other cities in the game as well, but they are “somewhere else on earth”. Even the New York stage isn’t a 1:1 reproduction of the real New York City and draws inspiration from a “mix of various cultures, like Madrid or Seoul”.

In other news, Capcom plans to use AI but will not implement generative AI materials in game content. Additionally, Saudi Arabia’s Electronic Gaming Development Company has acquired a 5% stake in Capcom. What are your thoughts on Pragmata devs making New York look AI-generated intentionally? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Kingdom Come Deliverance 2’s director speaking in support of NVIDIA DLSS 5. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Dying Light: The Beast Restored Land Finally Lets You Beat The Apocalypse
item.content:encoded:
Dying Light: The Beast is one of the most phenomenal open-world zombie-slaying experiences released in recent years. When it dropped last September, it earned top marks from Insider Gaming, and it went on to impress millions of gamers worldwide. But the game that brought Kyle Crane back to our screens is only just getting started.

Techland has just shadowdropped Dying Light: The Beast Restored Land, an all-new experience that boosts the game’s difficulty but makes it ultimately possible to rid Castor Woods of the zombie infection, once and for all.

You won’t want to miss this.

What is Dying Light: The Beast Restored Land?

Restored Land is a new Dying Light: The Beast experience that stands as a ‘side mode’ away from the core campaign platform. In Restored Land, everything is finite, from the resources and the side content to the undead enemies that roam Castor Woods.

That’s right – in Restored Land, you can actually eliminate every single zombie in the game. It’s all about taking back the environment from the cold grip of the undead, and everything has permanent consequences. When you loot a location, those resources are gone for good. Events that were once repeatable now become single actions.

And most importantly, when you kill zombies, they’ll stay dead, allowing you to recover regions from the infected and encourage survivors to return and resettle.

We caught up with Techland regarding Restored Land, and Tymon Smektala, the franchise director, said that the Restored Land update is immense in scope and scale:

It’s almost incomparable to the game that was released in September.

The game’s director, Nathan Lemaire, explained how it’s all about taking the world of Castor Words and flipping the script:

You are shaping the world as the player.

Restored Land is all about a gritty, realistic, and grounded experience. It’s more survival-based, which means you need to eat and drink, and tools like your torch will only work if you have enough batteries to power them. As you ‘Restore the Land’, you’ll find that enemies are tougher, resources are scarcer, but the game is much richer for it.

But there’s so much more than just that coming with Restored Lands.

This is a new SKU; a new version of the game. Techland has confirmed that Restored Lands, which will be free for existing owners of the game, will introduce:

  • Competitive vehicle-based races called Roadkill Rallies that are ‘destruction derby’ by nature
  • An option to enable One Life mode that wipes your save on death
  • Leaderboards in both Restored Land and Roadkill Rallies, to prove that you’re the best of the best
  • 33 new quest encounters, adding incredible depth to the game’s narrative
  • Additions to Co-op to contextualize actions and make it easier to collaborate
  • High-risk zombie encounters and improvements to Special Infected
  • 7 new Trophies/Achievements exclusive to Restored Lands

This is one hell of an update, and what’s better, it’s a side mode, which means you can play Restored Land while keeping your core save intact.

Dying Light: The Beast Restored Land is available right now on all platforms.

Are you excited to get back into Castor Woods with Restored Land? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that PS3 consoles just got an update

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
